title: Claude Code Agent Frontmatter Schema
description: Correctness gate for YAML frontmatter in agent .md files. Validates field types, enums, and patterns against Anthropic's documented Claude Code fields (March 2026). This schema is NOT a security boundary -- it cannot prevent prompt injection in descriptions, arbitrary commands in hooks, or malicious MCP server configs. Unrecognized fields are silently accepted (additionalProperties: true) because Claude Code ignores unknown fields at runtime. Governance metadata belongs in the companion .governance.yaml file, not here. Sources: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents, https://code.claude.com/docs/en/tools-reference, https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks.

name.description: Agent identifier. Kebab-case, lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens. Must not contain consecutive hyphens (--). Must not start or end with hyphen. Must not contain reserved words 'claude' or 'anthropic' (enforced by schema). Must be unique within scope (project > user > plugin). Example: 'my-agent-name'.

description.description: When Claude should delegate to this agent. Include 'use proactively' to encourage automatic invocation. This is the primary routing signal. No XML angle brackets (<>). Max 2048 chars to prevent context budget exhaustion. KNOWN ISSUE: Claude Code uses a simplified parser -- multiline YAML (>, |, >-) silently breaks agent discovery with no error (#4700). Use single-line or quoted strings only.

model.description: Model selection. Aliases: sonnet, opus, haiku, inherit, default. Full IDs: claude-opus-4-6, claude-sonnet-4-6, claude-haiku-4-5. Extended context: opus[1m], sonnet[1m]. Special: opusplan. Resolution: CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL env var > per-invocation > frontmatter > parent. Default: inherit.

oneOf.description: Comma-separated tool names. Example: 'Read, Write, Glob, Bash'

tools.description: Allowlist of tools. If omitted, agent inherits ALL tools from parent including MCP tools. Special syntax: Agent(name1, name2) restricts which subagents can be spawned. disallowedTools is applied first when both are set. NOTE: The subagent spawning tool was renamed from 'Task' to 'Agent' in v2.1.63. Both names work as aliases. Hook payloads use 'Agent' as tool_name (#29677).

disallowedTools.description: Denylist of tools to remove from inherited or specified set. Applied before tools allowlist. Tool names are PascalCase. Example: 'Bash, WebSearch, Write'. Common tools: Agent, Bash, Edit, Glob, Grep, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch, Write. MCP tools: mcp__<server>__<tool>. KNOWN LIMITATION: Restriction is name-based not capability-based -- agents with Bash access can bypass Write/Edit restrictions via shell commands (#31292). For true read-only enforcement, exclude Bash from tools.

oneOf.description: Server name reference. Reuses already-configured server from parent session.
Inline server definition. Key is server name, value is config object with type, command, args.
Official array format per Anthropic docs. Each entry is a string reference or inline {name: config} definition.
Full MCP server config or tool allowlist.

tools.description: Allowlist of specific MCP tools from this server.

oneOf.description: Legacy object format (key=server name, value=true or config). Functional at runtime. Used by Tom agents.

mcpServers.description: MCP servers available to this subagent. Official format: array of string references or inline definitions. Legacy format: object with server names as keys. Both accepted at runtime. String references share parent session connection; inline definitions are connected at subagent start, disconnected at finish. KNOWN ISSUE: Custom agents in .claude/agents/ may not receive MCP tools at runtime even when declared here (#25200). Verify MCP tool availability in your deployment.

permissionMode.description: Permission prompt behavior. KNOWN LIMITATION: If parent uses bypassPermissions, ALL subagents inherit it unconditionally -- this field cannot restrict permissions below the parent level (#20264). If parent uses auto mode, subagent inherits auto mode and this field is ignored.

maxTurns.description: Maximum agentic turns before agent stops.

skills.description: Skill names to preload. Full skill content injected into agent context at startup. Subagents do NOT inherit skills from parent conversation.

hooks.description: Lifecycle hooks scoped to this agent. Keys are event names, values are arrays of hook configs. Stop auto-converts to SubagentStop. Example: hooks: { PreToolUse: [{ type: command, command: './check.sh' }] }. Events: PreToolUse, PostToolUse, SubagentStart, SubagentStop, Stop, UserPromptSubmit, SessionStart, TaskCreated, TaskCompleted, PreCompact, PostCompact, and 14 more. Handler types: command, http, prompt, agent. KNOWN LIMITATION: Hooks defined in agent frontmatter are NOT executed when the agent runs as a subagent (#18392). All hook-based enforcement must be at session level.

memory.description: Persistent memory scope for cross-session learning. Creates memory directory: user at ~/.claude/agent-memory/<name>/, project at .claude/agent-memory/<name>/, local at .claude/agent-memory-local/<name>/. When enabled, Read/Write/Edit auto-enabled. First 200 lines or 25KB of MEMORY.md injected into system prompt.

background.description: Always run as background task. Background subagents auto-deny permissions not pre-approved. AskUserQuestion tool calls fail but subagent continues. Default: false.

effort.description: Reasoning depth when this agent is active. Overrides session effort level. 'max' is Opus 4.6 only. CLAUDE_CODE_EFFORT_LEVEL env var takes precedence. Default: inherits from session.

isolation.description: Run in temporary git worktree. Gives isolated copy of repository. Auto-cleaned if subagent makes no changes. KNOWN ISSUE: Context compaction during worktree execution can cause CWD drift and nested worktrees, potentially bypassing branch isolation (#27881).

initialPrompt.description: Auto-submitted as first user turn when agent runs as main session agent (--agent flag or agent setting). Commands (/skill-name) and skills are processed. Prepended to user-provided prompt. Only meaningful for --agent mode, not subagent invocations.

color.description: Background color for subagent UI identification. Used by /agents interactive command. Not in official frontmatter table. Known values: blue, purple, yellow, green, red. See github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/8501.
